sprint: The Kid's Journey -- First Screen to Publishing, With Real Screenshots
task: Documentation-only addition, no application code changed. Answers a direct question: 'do we have the journey of a kid documented from first screen till publishing with both roles visitor and creator?' Found the pieces existed but nothing current and unified -- docs/STUDIO_CREATION_JOURNEY_V1.md is canonical but predates Magic Card entirely; the Magic Card design doc has real Creator/Visitor Journey sections but was never committed and its own status table is stale. Built a new canonical docs/KID_JOURNEY.md with 25 real screenshots captured by driving the live app end-to-end via a new, repo-checked-in Playwright script (docs/journey/capture_screenshots.js + harness.js) -- genuinely re-runnable and updatable screen by screen, not a one-time snapshot. Covers Screen 1 through Screen 2, Workspace's Personalize/Add-Something/Refine states, all five Publish stages, the Awakening (the one screen where Visitor and Creator genuinely fork), the full Creator branch (claim, First Claimed Moment, header badge, Magic Card Home leading with the reveal-gated card per the prior sprint's fix), the full Visitor branch (identical Celebration, then a reboot proving no Identity Gate/badge ever appears), My Projects (works identically for both roles), a returning Creator's Identity Gate (single-card greeting and Shared Device picker), and Screen 1's two cross-device entry points (Vihu Card redemption vs. Magic Card recall). Every image reference cross-checked against the actual captured files with zero missing or orphaned images. Disclosed honestly: since Studio ships with zero built-in Worlds today, the walkthrough uses two small demo Worlds seeded into localStorage for capture only -- real product code and UI throughout, synthetic World content.
